text:
The paper titled "Multiple-Choice Prophet Inequalities" exists, but it belongs to **Brian Chen, David R. Karger, and Aleksander Madry** (a 2016 MIT course project/paper), not to "Robert Kleinberg and Sergei M. Krakovski" at SODA 2005. No paper with that exact title authored by Kleinberg and Krakovski from SODA 2005 was found anywhere. The relevant 2005/2007 work connecting prophet inequalities to mechanism design is by Hajiaghayi, Kleinberg, and Sandholm (titled "Automated Online Mechanism Design and Prophet Inequalities"), which is a different paper entirely. The author "Sergei M. Krakovski" does not appear in any of the search results, and the cited venue and year do not match the actual paper bearing this title.

VERDICT: LIKELY
EXPLANATION: The title "Multiple-Choice Prophet Inequalities" exists but belongs to Brian Chen, David Karger, and Aleksander Madry (2016), not to "Robert Kleinberg and Sergei M. Krakovski" at SODA 2005. No paper with this title and these authors was found in any source, and "Sergei M. Krakovski" does not appear in any relevant results.
LINK: https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/Multiple-Choice-Prophet-Inequalities-Chen-Karger/242b08107d5582fd1ccdf9975492c12ba383a3bd
